titles.ar: باب مَا يُبْسَطُ فِي اللَّحْدِ وَ وَضْعِ اللَّبِنِ وَ الْآجُرِّ وَ السَّاجِ
titles.en: Chapter 65 – What is levelled off in the chasm (Lahad), and placing of the slab, and the brick, and the wood

titles.ar: باب مَا يُبْسَطُ فِي اللَّحْدِ وَ وَضْعِ اللَّبِنِ وَ الْآجُرِّ وَ السَّاجِ
titles.en: Chapter 65 – What is levelled off in the chasm (Lahad), and placing of the slab, and the brick, and the wood

segments: كَتَبَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ بِلَالٍ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ رُبَّمَا مَاتَ الْمَيِّتُ عِنْدَنَا وَ تَكُونُ الْأَرْضُ نَدِيَّةً فَنَفْرُشُ الْقَبْرَ بِالسَّاجِ أَوْ نُطْبِقُ عَلَيْهِ فَهَلْ يَجُوزُ ذَلِكَ فَكَتَبَ ذَلِكَ جَائِزٌ .
arabic.text: كَتَبَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ بِلَالٍ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّهُ رُبَّمَا مَاتَ الْمَيِّتُ عِنْدَنَا وَ تَكُونُ الْأَرْضُ نَدِيَّةً فَنَفْرُشُ الْقَبْرَ بِالسَّاجِ أَوْ نُطْبِقُ عَلَيْهِ فَهَلْ يَجُوزُ ذَلِكَ فَكَتَبَ ذَلِكَ جَائِزٌ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ali Bin Muhammad Al Qasany who said,

‘Ali Bin Bilal wrote to Abu Al Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Sometimes someone dies with us and the ground happens to be damp, so we pave the grave with the wood, or we re-inforce upon it. Is that Permissible?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘That is allowed’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ali Bin Muhammad Al Qasany who said,

‘Ali Bin Bilal wrote to Abu Al Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Sometimes someone dies with us and the ground happens to be damp, so we pave the grave with the wood, or we re-inforce upon it. Is that Permissible?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘That is allowed’.

segments: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qasaniy who has said the following: “Once Ali ibn Bilal wrote to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the condition of a grave asking, ‘Someone may die in our area but the ground of the grave is wet.’ We pave it (the floor) with teak (wooden board) or cover it in this way, is this permissible? He (the Imam) wrote the answer, ‘It is permissible.’” H 4536, Ch. 64, h 2
en.sarwar.text: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from Ali ibn Muhammad al-Qasaniy who has said the following: “Once Ali ibn Bilal wrote to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the condition of a grave asking, ‘Someone may die in our area but the ground of the grave is wet.’ We pave it (the floor) with teak (wooden board) or cover it in this way, is this permissible? He (the Imam) wrote the answer, ‘It is permissible.’” H 4536, Ch. 64, h 2

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/102)
gradings.label: ضعيف على المشهور
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/102)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ الْقَاسَانِيِّ قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يُبْسَطُ فِي اللَّحْدِ وَ وَضْعِ اللَّبِنِ وَ الْآجُرِّ وَ السَّاجِ
titles.en: Chapter 65 – What is levelled off in the chasm (Lahad), and placing of the slab, and the brick, and the wood

segments: أَلْقَى شُقْرَانُ مَوْلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فِي قَبْرِهِ الْقَطِيفَةَ .
arabic.text: أَلْقَى شُقْرَانُ مَوْلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فِي قَبْرِهِ الْقَطِيفَةَ .

segments: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ja’far Bin Bashir, from Yahya Bin Abu Al A’ala,
from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The cotton spread was cast upon Shuqran, a slave of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, in his grave’.
en.hubeali.text:
Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Salih Bin Al Sindy, from Ja’far Bin Bashir, from Yahya Bin Abu Al A’ala,

from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The cotton spread was cast upon Shuqran, a slave of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>, in his grave’.

segments: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from ibn Muskan from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, placed bricks on the grave of the Holy prophet.’ I then asked, ‘If one places tiles on the grave is it harmful to the deceased?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not harmful.’”
en.sarwar.text: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from Husayn ibn ‘Uthman from ibn Muskan from Aban ibn Taghlib who has said the following: “I once heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘Ali, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, placed bricks on the grave of the Holy prophet.’ I then asked, ‘If one places tiles on the grave is it harmful to the deceased?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘No, it is not harmful.’”

gradings.raw: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/103)
gradings.label: مجهول
gradings.text: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (14/103)

narratorChain.raw: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنْ صَالِحِ بْنِ السِّنْدِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ بَشِيرٍ عَنْ يَحْيَى بْنِ أَبِي الْعَلَاءِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) قَالَ

titles.ar: باب مَا يُبْسَطُ فِي اللَّحْدِ وَ وَضْعِ اللَّبِنِ وَ الْآجُرِّ وَ السَّاجِ
titles.en: Chapter 65 – What is levelled off in the chasm (Lahad), and placing of the slab, and the brick, and the wood

segments: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ جَعَلَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَى قَبْرِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَبِناً فَقُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ جَعَلَ الرَّجُلُ عَلَيْهِ آجُرّاً هَلْ يَضُرُّ الْمَيِّتَ قَالَ لَا .
arabic.text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ جَعَلَ عَلِيٌّ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَى قَبْرِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) لَبِناً فَقُلْتُ أَ رَأَيْتَ إِنْ جَعَلَ الرَّجُلُ عَلَيْهِ آجُرّاً هَلْ يَضُرُّ الْمَيِّتَ قَالَ لَا .

segments: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Husayn Bin Usman, from Ibn Muskan, from Aban Bin Taghlib who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> made a slab to be upon the grave of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>’. So I said, ‘What is your<sup>asws</sup> view if the man were to make a brick to be upon him, would it harm the deceased?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.
en.hubeali.text:
Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Husayn Bin Usman, from Ibn Muskan, from Aban Bin Taghlib who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> made a slab to be upon the grave of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>’. So I said, ‘What is your<sup>asws</sup> view if the man were to make a brick to be upon him, would it harm the deceased?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No’.

narratorChain.raw: مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ الْحَكَمِ عَنْ حُسَيْنِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنِ ابْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ قَالَ
